80.reason: [Error: The loop condition `i<=num` causes an out-of-bounds access when `i` equals `num`, because `count[num]` is uninitialized (word array may contain garbage). This can lead to incorrect comparisons or crashes. It should be `i<num`.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  Loop condition should be i<num; when num=0, accessing count[0] uses uninitialized data, causing undefined behavior.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Error: should be i<num to avoid accessing uninitialized count[num].word]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  num is the count of already stored unique words. Using i<=num accesses count[num], whose word field contains uninitialized garbage, leading to undefined behavior in strcmp. It should be i < num.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Loop condition i<=num accesses count[num] which is uninitialized/empty. Should be i < num to only check existing words.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Loop condition should be i < num, not i <= num. When i == num, count[i] is uninitialized (since valid indices are 0 to num-1), leading to undefined behavior when accessing count[i].word.]
